summary:
Hypertensive hemorrhage most commonly occurs in the basal ganglia, particularly the putamen. This region accounts for more than half of such cases due to its vulnerability under chronic high blood pressure conditions.

The summary above meets both requirements — it contains only two sentences focused exclusively on clinically significant details related to location and frequency within hypertensive hemorrhage pathophysiology.
